brand.name: Michael Roach Creative
brand.tagline: Solutions to your creative and marketing needs

offer.what: Full-service creative and digital marketing agency offering content marketing, social media management, branding strategy, video/audio production, copywriting, digital advertising, WordPress consulting, digital signage, career development coaching, and workforce/training programs
offer.coreOutcome: Businesses become more visible and heard — gaining audience engagement and competitive differentiation — without the owner having to do the marketing themselves
offer.pricePoint: Custom/undisclosed; contact for pricing on all services. No publicly listed rates. DesignRush lists minimum budget and hourly rate as 'Inquire.'
offer.commitment: Mix of project-based and retainer; digital marketing packages imply ongoing engagements; training programs may be one-off

audience.icp: Small to medium-sized business owners and entrepreneurs (primarily Albany, NY region) who need marketing help but lack in-house expertise or bandwidth — typically 1–50 employees, service or retail businesses
painPoints: Struggle to create high-quality content consistently while running the business
Invisible online — not ranking, not posting, not engaging
Unclear brand story or value proposition
No time or skill for video, social, or digital ads
Career changers/executives needing personal brand clarity for job search or promotion
segments: SMB owners needing ongoing digital marketing (social, SEO, ads, content)
Entrepreneurs and executives wanting personal branding and career positioning
Nonprofits and organizations needing workforce/career readiness training
Local businesses needing digital signage or one-off creative projects

salesCycle.length: Weeks — discovery call → proposal → engagement letter
salesCycle.trial: Free consultation available; no formal free trial or first-session offer
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Contact form submission → free consultation call → custom proposal
salesCycle.topObjection: Cost/budget uncertainty — pricing is opaque and SMBs are often price-sensitive; second objection is whether an outside agency can really understand their specific business

ahaMoment: The first delivered content piece, campaign, or strategy deck that makes the client feel professionally represented — typically a polished social post, video, or brand document they couldn't have produced themselves

retention.recurring: Digital marketing retainer packages (social media management, ongoing content creation); repeat bookings for training programs
retention.expansion: Adding service lines (e.g., client starts with social → adds video production, email marketing, or digital ads); upsell to speaking engagements or workforce training contracts
retention.churnRisk: SMBs cutting marketing spend during slow seasons; single-person shop creates key-person dependency; no visible self-serve or community to maintain engagement between projects

lifecycleGaps: No visible welcome or onboarding email sequence for new leads or clients
Lead magnets (audio course, WP video) exist but no nurture sequence is apparent
No booking confirmation / reminder / no-show recovery system visible
No value newsletter to stay top-of-mind with past clients and warm leads
No renewal or re-engagement campaign for lapsed clients
No referral or advocacy program surfaced
toneVoice: Professional and approachable with understated confidence. Educational and accessible — explains concepts without jargon. Collaborative and encouraging ('Let's explore what you need'). Avoids hype; leans on credentials (PCM, Google Analytics Certified, AMA member, Citibank/NYSE/PBS experience) to build trust quietly.
compliance: Standard CAN-SPAM and GDPR requirements apply. No healthcare, finance, or legal services. Albany, NY–based so NY state consumer protection norms. No special compliance flags identified.
